design_philosophy.core_loop: Select weapon loadout → navigate scrolling stages (vertical/horizontal) → defeat boss → unlock new weapons → progress through 6-stage loop with escalating difficulty
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Strategic weapon selection against stage design constraints; balancing offensive power vs. defensive utility (weapons function as shields)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Pilot a damaged fighter recovering its lost arsenal to repel alien invasion across diverse planetary environments
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Eschews traditional shoot-em-up power-up collection; instead grants weapons predictably at stage completion, forcing pre-level strategic planning. Rejects one-hit-kill convention with two-hit damage system (hit disables weapon, second hit on weakened state destroys ship). Emphasizes weapon versatility and stage-appropriate loadout customization over reflexive reaction to drops.

difficulty.lives_system: Respawn immediately at death location; lose life on enemy fire hit or solid obstacle collision; direct enemy collision destroys ship instantly regardless of weapon state
difficulty.continues_system: Limited continues/credits available; game over when all lives exhausted
difficulty.checkpointing: Respawn checkpoints at death location within stages; no mid-stage checkpoints documented
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: players select three weapon types before each stage and can freely switch between them mid-level, allowing difficulty modulation based on loadout effectiveness against encountered stage hazards and boss patterns

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon acquisition system: players unlock one new weapon choice (standard/special/bomb type) at end of each of 6 stages, expanding armory. Weapons are not power-ups but strategic unlocks that persist across runs.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage completion gates weapon unlocks; no skill-based or conditional unlocking documented
replayability_hooks: Game loops after stage 6 completion with increased difficulty and denser enemy bullet patterns
Multiple weapon combinations per stage enable different approach strategies
Vertical vs. horizontal stage transitions require adaptive weapon selection

world_building_techniques: Planetary system setting (Illis) with named locations (Cumuluses, Tralieb, Urbanite, Cavern, Sector 3 Lava Planet, Armada fortress)
Stage progression traces fighter's journey through invaded worlds toward final fortress confrontation
Regional plot variations (Japanese 'Mother' vs. international 'Corliss' naming)
Visual stage theming reflects planetary/environmental characteristics

boss_design.philosophy: One boss per stage (6 total); boss encounters serve as progression gates requiring appropriate weapon selection from available loadout. Boss design integrates with stage-weapon interplay—certain bosses reward specific weapon choices.
notable_bosses: Stage 2 boss: features 'Cosmic Dance!' music (remixed by Masanori Adachi)

faction_indicators: Armada of Annihilation (invasive alien empire antagonist)
Illis system defense (implicit player faction recovering D117B fighter)
danger_signals: Alien invasion of peaceful Illis system
Escalating enemy density and bullet patterns in loop restarts
environmental_storytelling: Cloud-covered Cumuluses stage suggests atmospheric corruption
Space colony Tralieb implies civilian infrastructure under threat
Populated Urbanite stage indicates civilian casualty stakes
Progression toward Armada fortress as endgame convergence point
